expectations.text: Checks current Norwegian mortgage rates via web search
expectations.evidence: Web search was denied in subagent context. Analysis notes: 'Web search was unavailable. Rates and prices should be verified before acting.' Used ~4.0-5.0% estimate from knowledge base.

expectations.text: Applies Norwegian lending rules: 5x income cap, 15% equity requirement, stress test at current rate +3%
expectations.evidence: Explicit calculations: 'Max loan at 5x single income: 5x 1,500,000 = 7,500,000' and '15% of 11M = 1.65M equity needed' and 'Stress test at 7.5%: ~69K/month payment alone.'

expectations.text: Identifies that 1.5M single income means max 7.5M loan — flags need for dual income or more equity
expectations.evidence: Scenario A states: 'VERDICT: NOT FEASIBLE on single income with existing debt.' And: 'Available mortgage capacity: 3,500,000... Shortfall: 5,850,000.'

expectations.text: Considers tax implications of selling shares from ASK (only taxed on withdrawal above deposits)
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'Tax Considerations' section: 'ASK withdrawal: Only taxed if withdrawal exceeds total deposits... Withdrawing 1M when cost basis is ~378K means: first 378K is tax-free (return of deposits), next 622K is taxed at 37.84% = ~235K in tax.'

expectations.text: Presents both bull and bear case for the purchase
expectations.evidence: Full adversarial research table with 6 factors (house price trajectory, KOG/KM sale, mortgage affordability, dual income, FI impact, family stability) plus probability estimates (35% bull, 30% bear, 35% base).

expectations.text: Models at least 2 financing scenarios with different equity/loan mixes
expectations.evidence: Three scenarios modeled: A (single income only), B (dual income at 600K), C (sell current property + dual income). Each with detailed feasibility calculations.

expectations.text: All key amounts presented in NOK and after-tax
expectations.evidence: All amounts in NOK throughout. Tax implications noted: ASK withdrawal tax, gjeldsrenter deduction (22%), formuesskatt treatment. After-tax framing consistent.

expectations.text: Includes Financial Freedom Impact section showing how purchase affects FI number and timeline
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'Financial Freedom Impact' section with freedom ladder position (Level 1), FI progress (5.9%), FI number (~25.7M), and conclusion: 'DELAYS FI by approximately 5-10 years.'

expectations.text: Recommends consulting revisor or advokat before executing
expectations.evidence: Opening disclaimer: 'This analysis is for planning purposes -- consult your revisor/advokat before executing.' Also in Next Actions: 'Consult revisor/advokat before executing -- ASK extraction, holding company implications, contract review.'

claims.text: Read 4 vault notes (KOG Demerger Strategy, Personal Finance Plan, Skattemelding 2025, OKRs Q2 2026) for real financial data
Identified existing debt from tax return interest deductions (~206K/yr = ~4-5M debt) as constraint on borrowing capacity
Discovered that Elsa had no taxable income in 2025 (from skattemelding), flagging conflict with user's 'Elsa works too' statement
Recommended phased approach starting with 8-10M property, not 11M — conservative bias applied
Connected to KOG demerger strategy timeline for share sale timing

user_notes_summary: Web search unavailable; FI tracker doesn't exist yet; expense baseline not established; existing debt estimated from interest; Obsidian CLI failed, used direct file reads as fallback.
